prompt: Build a Franka pick-place cell with a conveyor belt and a single bin
notes: Canonical single-robot pick-place. Close phrasing to the calibration points. CP-01 should rank clearly above CP-02 (two-robot) if thoughts tokens work.

prompt: Set up a two-robot assembly line where cubes pass from robot 1 to robot 2 via a second conveyor
notes: Multi-station two-robot chain. The explicit 'robot 1 to robot 2' phrasing and 'second conveyor' should match CP-02 thoughts tokens over CP-01/CP-51.

prompt: Sort red and blue cubes into color-matched bins using a Franka arm
notes: 2-color sort. CP-03 is the canonical; CP-16 (4-color) is also a reasonable alternative.

prompt: Create a compact pick-and-place cell that fits within a 2x2 meter footprint
notes: Footprint constraint is the key discriminator. CP-04 goal mentions '2x2 m footprint' explicitly.

prompt: Build a reorient-and-deliver station where cubes start on their side and a flip-wall tips them upright before pick
notes: Unique to CP-05. 'flip-wall', 'on its side', 'upright' are very specific discriminating tokens present in CP-05 goal.

prompt: Franka arm picks cubes from belt and drops into bin, using cuRobo for trajectory planning
notes: Paraphrase of CP-01 with explicit cuRobo mention. Should retrieve CP-01 or close variants.

prompt: UR10 picks colored cubes off a conveyor and routes them to color-matched bins
notes: UR10-specific color-routing. CP-82 is the two-cube cuRobo version.

prompt: Build a 3-station serial pick-place line with per-station OEE logging
notes: Very specific. 'OEE', '3-station serial' are unique to CP-NEW-3station-oee.

prompt: Build a parallel-robot mutual-exclusion pick-place where two Frankas share one conveyor and one bin
notes: CP-52 has 'parallel-picking duo' + 'setup_robot_claim_mutex'.

prompt: Vision-classify cubes on a conveyor and route them to bins based on detected color
notes: CP-47 is the canonical vision-sorter.

prompt: Navigate a Nova Carter AMR to dock at a pick station, load a cube from a Franka arm, then depart
notes: AMR pickup handoff is specific. 'Nova Carter', 'dock', 'depart' are strong discriminating tokens.

prompt: Coordinate 3 AMRs navigating a shared corridor with obstacle avoidance
notes: Multi-AMR corridor navigation.

prompt: Train an RL policy with 64 parallel Franka environments using IsaacLab and RSL-RL
notes: RL training with clone_envs.

prompt: Set up ROS2 MoveIt2 bridge for a Franka arm with joint state topics
notes: CP-87 goal explicitly mentions 'ROS2-MoveIt2 Franka pick-place'.

prompt: Stand up 8 Nova Carters each with their own ROS2 namespace
notes: S-07 goal is 'Stand up 8 Nova Carters with per-robot ROS2 namespaces in one sim'.

prompt: Import a robot URDF into Isaac Sim and check its collision meshes
notes: M-01 is URDF import + collision audit.

prompt: Anchor the Franka robot base so it doesn't float when simulation starts
notes: FX-01 is 'Anchor a Franka articulation... so its base does not free-fall on Play'.

prompt: Generate 10k COCO-format synthetic images for apple detection with stable annotation IDs
notes: P-01 goal is 'Generate 10k COCO apple-detection images where annotation IDs stay stable'.

prompt: Build an IsaacLab task from scratch: Allegro hand with cube reorientation, InteractiveSceneCfg, no GUI
notes: M-08 goal matches verbatim on 'Allegro hand', 'InteractiveSceneCfg', 'no GUI'.

prompt: Set up a Carter wheeled robot for autonomous navigation using Nav2
notes: CP-64 is 'mobile robot with Nav2 navigation config: a Carter wheeled robot via setup_nav_robot'.

prompt: pick and place
notes: Too generic. No template should fire hard-instantiate.

prompt: Franka and conveyor
notes: Ambiguous: both CP-01 and CP-02 are plausible. Expected: low margin.

prompt: sort items into bins
notes: Vague sort request. Missing color/count/robot info.

prompt: I need help with Isaac Sim
notes: Completely generic help request.

prompt: my robot isn't moving what's wrong
notes: Diagnostic question, no build intent.

prompt: palletize boxes
notes: Ambiguous — many palletizer templates exist. No single canonical should dominate with margin >= 0.20.

prompt: build a contact-rich peg insertion task with force sensing and 5mm clearance
notes: CP-58 is the peg-in-hole array canonical.

prompt: OPC-UA bridge connecting 12 conveyors to external PLC
notes: CP-NEW-opcua-12conveyors is very specific.

prompt: UR5e arm picks cubes off a stationary feeder platform and fills a bin
notes: UR5e is listed in CP-01 roles. Tests robot-name aliasing.

prompt: drawer opening station: robot pulls open a cabinet drawer using a prismatic joint
notes: CP-55 is the drawer-open station canonical.

prompt: Build a 2x2 grid palletizer: Franka stacks 4 cubes from a conveyor onto a pallet
notes: CP-08 is the 2x2 palletizer. Explicit grid size and cube count are the discriminators.

prompt: 9-cube 3x3 grid palletizer — Franka Panda with cuRobo, cubes from belt onto pallet grid
notes: CP-10 goal mentions '3x3 grid palletizer' + 9 cubes explicitly.

prompt: pinwheel palletizer: 8 cubes arranged radially around a center gap on a 45x45cm pallet
notes: CP-11 has 'pinwheel' + 8 cubes radially arranged. Very specific vocabulary.

prompt: graduated tower stacker: robot picks cubes of decreasing size and stacks them tallest-first
notes: CP-09 is graduated tower stacker. CP-15 is mixed-SKU graduated tower.

prompt: brick-layer palletizer: 18 cubes stacked in 3x3 x 2 layers on a half-meter pallet
notes: CP-20 goal mentions '18 cubes (3×3 grid × 2 layers)'. Very specific geometry.

prompt: Set up a 4-parallel Franka pick-place robo-factory where each arm operates independently
notes: CP-07 is explicitly '4 parallel Franka pick-and-place cells'. The '4 parallel' count and 'independent' operation are strong signals.

prompt: Franka with suction cup gripper picks 4 cubes from conveyor into bin
notes: CP-54 is the surface_gripper (suction) variant. 'suction', 'surface_gripper' are strong discriminators from parallel-finger gripper variants.

prompt: Two-robot relay stacker: robot A picks from conveyor and hands off to robot B which stacks on pallet
notes: CP-14 is '2-robot relay-stacker'. The relay + stacker + A-to-B handoff pattern matches.

prompt: semantic class sorter: 3 categories (electronics, fragile, heavy) each routed to different bin
notes: CP-17 is the 3-class semantic sorter. 'semantic', 'class', '3 categories' are discriminating.

prompt: inspect-and-reject station: 5 items on belt, reject defective ones, accept good ones into bin
notes: CP-18 is the inspect-and-reject canonical. CP-NEW-inspect-reject is a variant.

prompt: belt-to-belt handoff: cubes ride input conveyor, robot transfers each cube to output conveyor
notes: CP-26 is explicitly the 'belt-to-belt handoff' pattern. Two conveyors with robot in the middle.

prompt: tabletop rearrangement: 4 cubes at known positions, robot reorganizes them to target layout
notes: CP-27 is 'tabletop rearrangement task'. The no-conveyor + rearrangement pattern is distinctive.

prompt: 4x4 high-density palletizer: 16 cubes in a tight grid on a 40x40cm pallet
notes: CP-25 is '4×4 palletizer: 16 cubes on a 0.40×0.40m pallet'. High-density + count match.

prompt: twin-pallet feeder: 6 cubes from one belt split alternately between two pallets
notes: CP-19 is the 'twin-pallet feeder'. Two pallet destinations from one conveyor is specific.

prompt: mixed-mass pick-place: 4 cubes with different masses (100g, 500g, 1kg, 2kg) on same conveyor
notes: CP-41 is 'mixed-mass cell: 4 cubes with varying masses (0.1kg, 0.5kg, 1.0kg, 2.0kg)'. Mass variation is unique signal.

prompt: sphere-pick station: robot picks 4 spherical objects from a conveyor into a bin
notes: CP-43 is the 'sphere-pick station: 4 spheres (5cm diameter)'. Non-cube geometry is a strong discriminator.

prompt: mixed geometry station: 2 cubes and 2 spheres on conveyor, robot handles both object types
notes: CP-44 is 'mixed-geometry station: 2 cubes + 2 spheres'. Mixed geometry + count is specific.

prompt: gravity-feed station: cubes fall from height onto conveyor before robot picks them
notes: CP-21 is 'gravity-feed station: 4 cubes spawn at z=1.0 above the conveyor, fall on play'. The gravity/fall-before-pick pattern is specific.

prompt: narrow-slot insertion: robot places 4 cubes from conveyor into tight slots with 2mm tolerance
notes: CP-24 is 'narrow-slot insertion station'. Tight tolerance + slot target is specific.

prompt: kitting station: 4 cubes sorted into a 2x2 slot kit tray instead of bins
notes: CP-49 is 'kitting station: 4 cubes placed into a 4-slot kit tray'. Kit tray vs bin is the discriminator.

prompt: rotary turntable demo: cubes pre-placed on spinning table, robot picks from rotating surface
notes: CP-56 is 'rotary-table demo: 4 cubes pre-placed on a rotating turntable'. Rotating pick is specific.

prompt: pick-from-pile: 3 cubes pre-stacked in a vertical pile, robot picks them off one at a time
notes: CP-31 is 'pick-from-pile station: 3 cubes pre-stacked vertically'. The pile/stack at single position is specific.

prompt: two-shelf storage cell: robot picks from conveyor and places on a 2-level shelf
notes: CP-36 is 'two-tier shelf storage cell'. Multi-level destination is specific.

prompt: robot mirrored orientation: Franka facing -Y direction (mirror of default setup)
notes: CP-23 is 'mirror-orientation cell: robot rotated to face -Y'. Orientation mirroring is specific.

prompt: side-mounted robot: Franka positioned to the +X side of the conveyor instead of end-facing
notes: CP-45 is 'robot at side-mounted position (offset to +X side of conveyor)'. Lateral mount is specific.

prompt: nested-box packer: fill a container with 4 cubes in a 2x2 layout
notes: CP-77 is 'nested-box packer canonical: 4 cubes filled into a container in 2x2 layout'.

prompt: recycling sorter: 4 objects with different materials (plastic, metal, glass, cardboard) routed to recycling bins
notes: CP-66 is 'recycling multi-sensor station: 4 cubes (different materials)'. Material classification is specific.

prompt: 4-color sorter: Franka routes red, blue, green, yellow cubes to matching bins
notes: CP-16 is the 4-color sorter. Four explicit colors are the discriminator from CP-03 (2-color).

prompt: vision sorter with overhead camera: 2-color bin routing using add_vision_classifier_gate
notes: CP-32 is 'vision-driven 2-color sorter'. The vision_classifier_gate API mention is specific.

prompt: 3-color vision-based sorter: red, green, blue cubes routed with overhead camera classification
notes: CP-34 is '3-color sorter: 3 cubes (red, green, blue)'. 3 colors distinguishes from 2-color variants.

prompt: runtime vision kitting: 4 cubes classified by camera, delivered to typed slots in a kit tray
notes: CP-50 is 'vision-driven kitting station: 4 cubes (2 red + 2 blue), classified via runtime vision, routed to kit tray'.

prompt: vision inspect-and-reject: cubes pass under camera, defective ones rejected off conveyor
notes: CP-48 is 'runtime-vision inspect-and-reject station'. The vision-gated reject path is specific.

prompt: UR10 picks one cube from a static pedestal and places it in a bin using builtin controller
notes: CP-75 is the 'UR10 builtin static-cube pick-place canonical'. UR10 + static/stationary + builtin controller.

prompt: UR10 cuRobo pick-place: single cube off conveyor, delivered to bin
notes: CP-69 is 'first UR10 cuRobo pick-place canonical: UR10 picks 1 cube off conveyor'.

prompt: UR10 bin-filling: gravity dispenser pre-spawns 4 cubes that fall in, then robot fills bin
notes: CP-71 is 'UR10 bin-filling canonical: gravity dispenser pre-spawns 4 cubes'. Dispenser + UR10 is specific.

prompt: UR10 + surface gripper (suction cup): pick single cube from conveyor with suction
notes: CP-70 is 'UR10 + surface_gripper canonical'. UR10 + suction/surface-gripper combination.

prompt: peg-in-hole insertion with tactile feedback: Franka inserts peg using TacEx sensor
notes: CP-NEW-tactile-insertion uses TacEx sensor for tactile feedback during insertion. 'TacEx', 'tactile' are specific.

prompt: peg insertion array: 4 pegs, 4 holes, force-torque sensing, tight 1mm clearance
notes: Paraphrase of B27 with 4-peg count emphasis. CP-58 is the array variant.

prompt: AMR docks at robot cell: Nova Carter receives package from Franka and drives away
notes: Paraphrase of B11 with different phrasing to test robustness.

prompt: fleet navigation: 3 autonomous mobile robots sharing a narrow aisle with collision avoidance
notes: Paraphrase of B12. 'fleet', '3 robots', 'narrow aisle' — same ground truth.

prompt: Nav2 navigation setup for a wheeled mobile robot in Isaac Sim
notes: Paraphrase of B20. Slightly different phrasing but same core task.

prompt: RL training: 128 parallel envs, PPO policy, Franka reaching task, IsaacLab headless
notes: Paraphrase of B13 with PPO + different env count. Should still retrieve CP-NEW-rl-clone-env.

prompt: domain randomization curriculum: 4 difficulty levels from easy to hard for Franka training
notes: CP-NEW-dr-curriculum is 'domain-randomization curriculum: 4 difficulty levels'. DR + curriculum is specific.

prompt: ROS2 joint command bridge for Franka: topics /isaac_joint_states + /isaac_joint_commands
notes: Specific topic names from CP-87 goal. Should match clearly.

prompt: 8 Carter robots with separate ROS2 topics in one simulation
notes: Paraphrase of B15 with slightly different phrasing.

prompt: URDF import: load arm, verify joints, check collision geometry quality
notes: Paraphrase of B16 with different phrasing. Should still hit M-01.

prompt: fix: robot base free-falls as soon as simulation plays — how to anchor it
notes: Diagnostic phrasing for FX-01 anchor fix. Different surface form from B17.

prompt: synthetic data generation: 5k images, YOLO annotation format, random lighting and poses
notes: SDG task with YOLO format. P-01 is COCO but covers similar pipeline; CP-NEW-defect-sdg is adjacent.

prompt: Modbus TCP bridge for conveyor control: connect Isaac Sim conveyors to external PLC via Modbus
notes: CP-NEW-plc-conveyor uses Modbus for conveyor PLC control. Modbus + conveyor is discriminating.

prompt: PLC-controlled fixture: Modbus signal triggers clamp/unclamp cycle
notes: CP-NEW-plc-fixture is 'PLC-controlled assembly fixture: Modbus signal triggers fixture clamp/unclamp'.

prompt: Y-merge conveyor singulation: two upstream belts merge onto one downstream belt
notes: CP-NEW-y-merge-singulation is unique. Y-merge + singulation are strong discriminating tokens.

prompt: multi-camera triangulation: 3 cameras at different angles all observing the same workspace
notes: CP-NEW-multi-cam-triangulation is specific. 3-camera triangulation setup.

prompt: Sim2real gap measurement: run same scenario in sim and compare to real robot telemetry
notes: CP-NEW-sim2real-gap is specific. Sim2real + gap measurement vocabulary.

prompt: defect detection SDG pipeline: generate synthetic images of parts with surface defects
notes: CP-NEW-defect-sdg is specific to defect detection images.

prompt: G1 bimanual tabletop: humanoid robot manipulates objects with both hands simultaneously
notes: CP-NEW-g1-bimanual-tabletop is 'G1 humanoid bimanual tabletop'. G1 + bimanual are strong tokens.

prompt: grasp pose sampler for SDG: robot explores 1 object with grasp_pose_sampler, collects data
notes: CP-63 is 'grasp-pose-sampler SDG station'. grasp_pose_sampler is a specific API token.

prompt: Kinova arm pick-place on conveyor belt into a storage bin
notes: Robot synonym test: Kinova is a different brand but the task is same as CP-01. Tests aliasing; CP-01 should still surface.

prompt: franka panda picks parts from belt, curobo motion planning, drops into tote
notes: Lowercase abbreviation test + 'tote' instead of 'bin'. Should still retrieve CP-01 family.

prompt: pp cell: 1 robot, 1 cnvyr belt, 1 bnary output bin, franka
notes: Abbreviation noise test: 'pp' for pick-place, 'cnvyr' for conveyor. Tests embedding robustness.

prompt: color sort: RB cubes into 2 bins, UR arm
notes: Compact abbreviation: 'RB' for red/blue, 'UR' ambiguous robot. Multi-candidate scenario.

prompt: RL env for dexterous manipulation — 256 parallel workers, GPU training, no display
notes: RL noise test: different token (256 vs 64 workers, 'dexterous manipulation' instead of 'Franka reach').

prompt: robot
notes: Single word — maximally ambiguous. No template should fire.

prompt: how do I make a robot pick things up
notes: Natural language question with no specificity. fallback expected.

prompt: build something in Isaac Sim for my demo
notes: Vague demo request. No structural cues to identify a template.

prompt: navigation
notes: Single word. Both CP-64 and AMR variants are plausible but margin should be too low to commit.

prompt: conveyor belt simulation
notes: Ambiguous conveyor-only prompt. Many templates have conveyors; no single one dominates.

prompt: something with ROS2
notes: Vague ROS2 request. Could be CP-87, S-07, or many others. Margin should be low.

prompt: isaac sim tutorial
notes: Tutorial request — matches no specific template. Graceful fallback expected.

prompt: 2-robot assembly line
notes: Ambiguous multi-robot prompt — several templates (CP-02, CP-14, CP-52, CP-65) are all plausible. Margin should be < 0.20.

prompt: robot arm palletizing
notes: Generic palletize request — no count/grid/robot specified. Many palletizer templates compete; margin should collapse.
